findings: Right lower lobe consolidation, consistent with pneumonia.
Normal cardiac silhouette size and contour.
Normal diaphragmatic position and costophrenic angles (left side clear).
impression: Right lower lobe pneumonia with possible associated pleural effusion.
differential_diagnoses: Lung abscess (less likely given acute presentation)
recommendations: Clinical correlation with symptoms and laboratory findings.
Consider follow-up imaging if clinical response is inadequate.
